resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def maximumTripletValue(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        max_val = float('-inf')
        for i in range(n - 2):
            for j in range(i + 1, n - 1):
                for k in range(j + 1, n):
                    max_val = max(max_val, (nums[i] - nums[j]) * nums[k])
        return max_val if max_val > 0 else 0
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^3)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution has a high efficiency due to its direct approach of checking all possible triplets. However, it has a high time complexity of O(n^3) due to the nested loops. The space complexity is O(1) as it only uses a constant amount of space. The energy implications are medium due to the cubic time complexity.
